issue: stale: 200k token figure outdated — Claude Sonnet/Opus 4.6+ now have 1M context window GA at standard pricing (Mar 2026)
new_a: When your knowledge base fits in the model\'s context window (Claude Sonnet/Opus 4.6+ ship a 1M-token window GA at standard pricing) — full-context + prompt caching is often faster and cheaper than building retrieval infra. Also skip RAG when answers don\'t depend on private or fresh data.

issue: stale: same 200k figure; duplicates fc-rag-1 logic — update to reflect 1M GA
new_q: When should you use full-context loading over RAG?
new_a: When the knowledge base fits in the model\'s context window (Claude Sonnet/Opus 4.6+ support 1M tokens GA in 2026) and prompt caching is available — you get a simpler architecture, often lower cost via cache reads at ~10% of input price, and zero retrieval-miss risk.

issue: context-missing: "ADO framework" is a custom acronym a candidate cold-reading the card cannot decode
new_q: ADO framework for handling an angry-customer / outage moment?
new_a: Acknowledge the impact in their words, Diagnose visibly (think out loud so they see progress), Own the resolution timeline with a concrete next checkpoint. Never blame the customer\'s environment in the moment.

issue: context-missing: "FDE" undefined — Forward Deployed Engineer is Palantir-specific jargon
new_q: STAR weighting for Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) behavioral rounds?
new_a: Situation: 2-3 sentences MAX. Task: clarify YOUR ownership (FDE rounds probe individual accountability). Action: ~60% of the story, focus on technical + customer decisions you made. Result: both technical outcome and quantified business impact.

new_q: Five must-have stories for a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) loop?
new_a: (1) Production fix under live customer pressure. (2) Pushing back on a client request. (3) Owning a deployment failure end-to-end. (4) Explaining a hard technical limit to a non-technical stakeholder. (5) Making a decision with incomplete information.

issue: context-missing: "decomposition round" is non-standard interview jargon
new_q: What is the #1 instant-reject signal in a problem-decomposition / case interview?
new_a: Jumping to specific tools or solutions before asking clarifying questions and scoping the problem (users, success metric, constraints, data).

issue: stale: OAuth 2.1 is the 2026 baseline — mandates PKCE for ALL clients (not just SPAs) and removes implicit + password grants
new_a: Authorization Code + PKCE is now required for ALL clients (web, SPA, mobile, native — OAuth 2.1 removed the public/confidential distinction for PKCE). Client Credentials for server-to-server. Device Code for TV/CLI. Implicit and Resource Owner Password Credentials grants are removed.

issue: stale/inaccurate: "~80%" figure isn\'t supported by current research; Cemri et al. 2025 taxonomy puts coordination/inter-agent failures around 37%, context loss specifically ~3%
new_a: Inter-agent coordination failures dominate (~37% of multi-agent bug reports per Cemri et al. 2025), and the biggest sub-pattern is the receiving agent missing context the sender assumed it had — wrong assumptions, dropped history, mismatched reasoning vs action. Fix: full trajectory logging, explicit state passing, and shared scratchpads instead of implicit handoffs.

issue: inaccurate: Voyage is an API service (acquired by MongoDB 2024) — not a local/VPC-deployable option without their managed-cloud / private-deploy contract; grouping it with BGE misleads
new_a: Self-hosted open-source models — BGE-M3 (Apache 2.0), Nomic Embed, GTE — run inside the VPC with no egress. API-based embeddings (OpenAI, Cohere, Voyage) need outbound network calls, so they\'re only an option if the customer accepts a private-deployment contract that runs the provider\'s model inside their VPC.

issue: inaccurate: Bridge pattern is shared DB instance with SEPARATE schemas per tenant — the card has it backwards ("shared schema, per-tenant DB")
new_a: Silo: dedicated DB per tenant (max isolation, highest cost). Pool: shared DB + shared schema with a tenant_id column (cheapest, noisy-neighbor risk). Bridge: shared DB instance with a separate schema per tenant (middle ground — logical isolation, easier per-tenant migrations). Match the pattern to the customer\'s compliance posture.

issue: inaccurate: same Bridge mis-definition as fc-sd-2; also duplicates fc-sd-2 — fix the definition
new_a: (1) Pool — shared DB, shared schema, tenant_id column. (2) Silo — dedicated DB per tenant. (3) Bridge — shared DB, separate schema per tenant. Match isolation level to compliance posture and blast-radius tolerance.

issue: minor: "in 2026" framing is fine but the answer should name the canonical cause (non-associative float reduction in batched matmul) for interview credibility
new_a: Floating-point math is non-associative, and batched GPU inference reorders reductions depending on batch size and kernel scheduling — so identical inputs at T=0 can produce slightly different logits and occasionally different tokens. Treat T=0 as "very stable," not "deterministic"; always validate output and retry-on-invalid.

issue: stale: in 2026 the standard pattern is native structured outputs / tool-use schemas + extended-thinking blocks, not regex-parsing <thinking>/<answer> tags
new_a: Use the provider\'s native structured outputs or tool-use schema (Anthropic extended thinking, OpenAI Structured Outputs) so reasoning lives in a separate thinking block and the final answer is schema-validated JSON. Reasoning is logged for debugging; only the validated JSON is returned to users.
